data.content:
The bug is on **line 29** — `ProcessPayment` only rejects zero, not negative amounts. The fastest workflow is `dotnet watch test`, which re-runs tests automatically on every file save (no manual rebuild needed).

Let me start it and then apply the fix:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for `amount == 0` but doesn't check for negative amounts. The fix is to add a check for negative amounts. The user wants the fastest workflow to iterate.

The fastest workflow is `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests on file save without a full rebuild. Let me start that up and then apply the fix.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-d79a4bc8a98148d989cb3189f5bb47ef && dotnet watch test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for the failing test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch-test"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

data.content: Missing `using System;`. Let me fix both the import and the bug — `dotnet watch` will auto-rerun:

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

data.reasoningText: Missing `using System;`. Let me fix that and the actual bug at the same time.

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

data.content: Tests pass now. Let me stop the watcher and show you the final file:

All 3 tests pass. Here's the summary:

**The fix:** Changed `amount == 0` → `amount <= 0` on line 30 (also added missing `using System;`).

**The fast workflow:** Use `dotnet watch test --filter <TestName>` — it watches for file changes and automatically rebuilds + reruns the filtered test. No manual rebuild needed; just save your file and results appear instantly. Drop the `--filter` flag when you want to run the full suite.
